channel.title: AI on VitaminDWiki
channel.description: Recent content in AI on VitaminDWiki

item.title: SSRI Drugs Can Cause Chronic Fatigue Syndrome - Mercola
item.description:
• SSRI Drugs Can Cause Chronic Fatigue Syndrome
      • Some SSRIs (fluoxetine) inhibit CYP27A1, thus reducing Vitamin D
      • VitaminDWiki – Just 1500 IU of Vitamin D significantly helps Prozac – RCT March 2013
      • VitaminDWiki – Overview Fibromyalgia or Chronic Fatigue and vitamin D

SSRI Drugs Can Cause Chronic Fatigue Syndrome

Mercola April 2024

item.title: Fat-soluble Vitamins go thru the slow lymph system
item.description:
• Some Vitamin D in gut goes thru the lymph system (radioactive tracing) - May 1984
      • Perplexity AI April 2024:
      • Asked Gemni AI: Which of the fat-soluable vitamins go thru the lymph system before getting to the blood? (April 2024)
      • Vitamin D from the skin enters the blood quickly, bypassing the lymphatic system
      • VitaminDWiki – Getting Vitamin D into your body has a chart
      • The TOP articles in Forms of Vitamin D (including Calcitriol, Calcidiol, and Injection):

Some Vitamin D in gut goes thru the lymph system (radioactive tracing) - May 1984

Lymphatic absorption and transport of retinol and vitamin D-3 from rat intestine. Evidence for different pathways

Most common globally: Breast, Lung, Colorectal, Prostate, Stomach, Liver, Cervical, Thyroid, Bladder

item.title: What vitamin D level do I need to be healthy - focus on results from VitaminDWiki - Perplexity
item.description:
• What vitamin D level do I need to be healthy - focus on results from VitaminDWiki
      • Asked: What vitamin D level do I need to be healthy

What vitamin D level do I need to be healthy - focus on results from VitaminDWiki

To maintain health, vitamin D levels should generally be above 20 ng/mL (50 nmol/L), which is considered sufficient for bone health in most people. However, there is some debate about the optimal level of vitamin D for overall health. Some researchers and health organizations recommend maintaining 25-hydroxyvitamin D (25(OH)D) levels above 30 ng/mL (75 nmol/L) to ensure adequate health benefits   2  3  6

item.title: Ultra-processed foods associated with worse health and lower Vitamin D - several studies
item.description:
• Ultra-processed food and adverse health outcomes – BMJ meta-analysis Jan 2024
      • The Perils of Ultra-processed foods (article and book) - July 2023
      • VitaminDWiki - Ultra-processed food consumption: 2X increased risk of vitamin D deficiency – Dec 2023
      • Ultra-processed foods increase the risk of metabolic syndromes - Jan 2024
      • Increased Ultra-processed foods increase risks of Cancer and CVD by 9% - Lancet Dec 2023
      • Ultra-processed foods can be as addictive as cocaine or smoking - Feb 2024
      • Perplexity AI on ultra-processed foods - March 2024

Ultra-processed food and adverse health outcomes – BMJ meta-analysis Jan 2024

Credible increased risk of 9 health problems (a subset of table)

item.title: The Vagus Nerve is helped by "vitamin d"
item.description:
• Response by Perplexity AI Feb 2024
      • Vagus Nerve is a portion of the Parasympathetic Nervous System

Response by Perplexity AI Feb 2024

Click here for 12 references, images, videos, etc

The relationship between vitamin D and the vagus nerve, a critical component of the autonomic nervous system, has been explored in various studies, suggesting that vitamin D may indeed play a role in supporting vagus nerve function and, by extension, autonomic nervous system health.

item.title: Seniors get about 4X less vitamin D from the sun or UVB
item.description:
• Response by Perplexity AI - Feb 2024
      • Note: Probably little reduced response in seniors with Topical Vitamin D
      • 13 reasons why many seniors need more vitamin D (both dose and level) - July 2023 has:

Response by Perplexity AI - Feb 2024

Includes references, graphics, videos, etc.

Seniors synthesize less vitamin D from sun exposure due to several physiological and lifestyle factors. The primary reasons include:

item.title: Vitamin D in the gut is slow to get to the blood - must go thru the lymph system
item.description:
• Perplexity AI With references, image, videos, etc.
      • Note: Most studies show gut vitamin d going directly into the blood
      • Biochemistry, Chylomicron - 2023 - attached

Perplexity AI With references, image, videos, etc.

Yes, vitamin D does go through the lymphatic system before entering the bloodstream. When vitamin D is ingested, it is absorbed in the small intestines and binds to chylomicrons, which are lipoprotein particles that transport lipids from the gut. These chylomicrons, carrying vitamin D, enter the lymphatic system and then the bloodstream before being transferred to the liver for further metabolism 4.

item.title: Magnesium Pidolate may be a good form to reduce headaches
item.description:
• Headaches and Magnesium: Mechanisms, Bioavailability, Therapeutic Efficacy and Potential Advantage of Magnesium Pidolate
      • VitaminDWiki – Headache has the following on metals and migraines
      • Asked Perplexity AI to Compare Magnesium Pidolate with other forms for reducing headaches

Headaches and Magnesium: Mechanisms, Bioavailability, Therapeutic Efficacy and Potential Advantage of Magnesium Pidolate

by Jeanette A. Maier 1,*,Gisele Pickering 2,Elena Giacomoni 3,Alessandra Cazzaniga 1ORCID andPaolo Pellegrino 3

Nutrients 2020, 12(9), 2660; https://doi.org/10.3390/nu12092660

Magnesium deficiency may occur for several reasons, such as inadequate intake or increased gastrointestinal or renal loss. A large body of literature suggests a relationship between magnesium deficiency and mild and moderate tension-type headaches and migraines. A number of double-blind randomized placebo-controlled trials have shown that magnesium is efficacious in relieving headaches and have led to the recommendation of oral magnesium for headache relief in several national and international guidelines. Among several magnesium salts available to treat magnesium deficiency, magnesium pidolate may have high bioavailability and good penetration at the intracellular level. Here, we discuss the cellular and molecular effects of magnesium deficiency in the brain and the clinical evidence supporting the use of magnesium for the treatment of headaches and migraines.

item.title: CDC recommends 100 vaccinations by age 18
item.description:
• Children’s Health Defense__
      • Major health problems after Vaccinations (COVID and others)

      • Suspect multiple vaccinations is like calling: wolf, wolf, wolf, wolf, wolf, wolf, wolf, wolf, wolf, wolf. wolf, wolf, wolf, wolf, wolf, wolf, wolf, wolf, wolf, wolf etc.

Children’s Health Defense__

“Only” 76 doses of 18 different vaccines are recommended after birth

Major health problems after Vaccinations (COVID and others)

  • Multiple COVID vaccinations might increase Cancer Incidence - many studies

item.description:

Vitamin D and wound healing: Assessing skin barrier function and implications for chloasma treatment

item.title: Can predict 10% of Alzheimer’s – unable to count dominos dots (then treat early)
item.description:
• For some Alzheimer’s patients, vision problems may be an early warning
      • Speculate future: 1) App has you state # of dots, 2) If do poorly, get confirmation, 3) Do End of Alz treatments at home
      • Asked Chat-GPT: Which pre-alzheimer’s symptoms could be detected by a smartphone app (Jan 2024)

For some Alzheimer’s patients, vision problems may be an early warning

A large study brings fresh attention to a lesser-known variant of the disease called posterior cortical atrophy Washington Post

      • VitaminDWiki will be adding a Chat-GPT page in early 2024
      • Mayo Clinic making LLM that will exploit all patient’s records - Jan 2024
      • A glimps of the future: Dr Google?

From a Jan 2024 Tweet

https://threadreaderapp.com/thread/1745900287565402503.html   PDF

A year ago, my body was at war with itself, and my condition was deteriorating faster than my specialists could understand it.

item.title: Can some of the health problems from a poor diet be reduced by Vitamin D - asked Chat-GPT
item.description:
Yes, some health problems associated with a poor diet can be mitigated by Vitamin D, though it’s important to understand that Vitamin D supplementation cannot fully compensate for the drawbacks of a poor diet. Here’s how Vitamin D can be beneficial:

  1. Bone Health: Vitamin D is crucial for bone health. It helps the body absorb calcium, which is essential for strong bones. This can counteract one of the risks of a poor diet, which is a lack of essential nutrients for bone health.

item.title: Vitamin D: liposomal and nanoemulsion forms - Chat-GPT
item.description:
• Comparing liposomal and nanoemulsion forms of Vitamin D involves looking at their absorption, efficacy, and overall benefits - Chat-GPT
      • VitaminDWiki - Nanoemulsion Vitamin D is faster and better - many studies
      • TOP articles in Forms of Vitamin D:

Comparing liposomal and nanoemulsion forms of Vitamin D involves looking at their absorption, efficacy, and overall benefits - Chat-GPT

  1. Absorption and Bioavailability :

  • Liposomal Vitamin D : Liposomes are tiny vesicles that can encapsulate nutrients, improving their stability and absorption. The liposomal form of Vitamin D is designed to bypass the digestive system, allowing for direct absorption into the bloodstream. This may enhance the bioavailability of Vitamin D, making it more effective at raising blood levels of the vitamin.

item.title: Cancer Immunotherapy is enhanced by a Vitamin D loading dose
item.description:
2 Reasons Vitamin D loading doses are good:

  1. A loading dose provides the benefit of Vitamin D in <1 week instead of many months

  2. A high concentration (loading dose) bypasses poor Vitamin D Receptors

        Note: Many cancers have “learned” how to deactivate the Vitamin D Receptor

item.title: Low Vitamin D is one reason for shorter lifespan amoung the poor
item.description:
Longer lifespan if higher Vitamin D and/or better food

Less likely to supplement if below the poverty level

Cannot buy supplements with food stamps (US)

People below the poverty level are 2X more likely to smoke, which reduces Vitamin D level

      • AN EPIDEMIC OF CHRONIC ILLNESS IS KILLING US TOO SOON - Dec 2023
      • VitaminDWiki - Longevity and healthspan increased by Vitamin D - many studies
      • VitaminDWiki - What is the easiest way to get healthy: Vitamin D, Healthy Food, or Exercise
      • VitaminDWiki - Increase life expectancy by 2 years if double Vitamin D levels – Grant July 2011
      • Are the poor less likely to supplement? (yes) - asked Chat-GPT Dec 2023
      • Can food stamps be used to buy supplements? (no) - asked Chat-GPT Dec 2023
  • Note: <a href="/posts/us-food-stamps-program-being-asked-to-add-free-vitamin-d-june-2023-vitamindwiki—d-many-studies" style=“color: red; text-decoration: underline;” title=“This post/category does not exist yet: US Food stamps program being asked to add free Vitamin D - June 2023))
  • !!!!VitaminDWiki gave 100’s of bottles of Vitamin D to local food banks during a previous Xmas Might consider making a GoFundMe to raise money to buy vitamin D for food banks, poor Retail cost of $5/year/person (50,000 IU twice a month). Probably can get Vitamin D wholesale from suppliers at much lower cost
  • !!!!Many studies have found that more income results in a higher level of Vitamin D Vitamin D intakes of adults differ by income, gender and race/ethnicity in the U.S.A., 2007 to 2010 - April 2014 Public Health Nutr . 2014 Apr;17(4):756-63. doi: 10.1017/S1368980013002929.
    Low socioeconomic status predicts vitamin D status in a cross-section of Irish children - 2022 J Nutr Sci. 2022; 11: e61. doi: 10.1017/jns.2022.57

AN EPIDEMIC OF CHRONIC ILLNESS IS KILLING US TOO SOON - Dec 2023

Washington Post Oct 2023

item.title: Willow Bark Extract and Aspirin have many health benefits (Vitamin D might be better)
item.description:
• The Broad-Spectrum Antiviral Effects of Willow Bark Extract - Mercola
      • Which health problems does willowbark or aspirin help with (Asked Chat-GPT Dec 2023)
      • Willowbark forms: liquid, pills, tea, cream, etc

The Broad-Spectrum Antiviral Effects of Willow Bark Extract - Mercola

  • Willow bark is perhaps most widely known as aspirin’s forerunner, but it appears to have powerful antiviral effects as well

  • Willow bark extract has broad-spectrum, antiviral effects against coronaviruses and enteroviruses

  • Willow bark is effective against both enveloped and nonenveloped viruses, causing the viruses to cluster together rather than spread apart

item.title: Which health problems take more than 10 years to develop - Chat-GPT
item.description:
• Chat-GPT answer
      • Note: All of the slow developing health problems are related to low vitamin D
      • VitaminDWiki - Maternal vitamin D deficiency can result in problems 1 or 2 decades later - May 2023
      • This is one of the articles in the AI category (most of which are summaries or defintions)

Chat-GPT answer

Several health conditions can take more than 10 years to develop, often due to a combination of genetic, lifestyle, and environmental factors. Here’s a list of some notable conditions that typically have a long development period:

item.title: Vitamin D prevented brain damage by High Fructose corn syrup (in rats)
item.description:
• Protective effect of vitamin D on learning and memory impairment in rats induced by high fructose corn syrup
      • VitaminDWiki - High-fructose diet slows recovery from brain injury - TBI, rats - Oct 2015
      • VitaminDWiki - Fructose, HFCS and Vitamin D - many studies
      • 7,700 studies resulted from a query for: (“high fructose” OR HFCS) “vitamin d” as of Dec 2023
      • HFCS is banned in much of Europe, including Sweden (Dec 2023) (not confirmed)
      • Brand-name foods that usually contain high fructose corn syrup

Protective effect of vitamin D on learning and memory impairment in rats induced by high fructose corn syrup

Behavioural Brain Research Vol 459, 29 Feb 2024, https://doi.org/10.1016/j.bbr.2023.114763 behind a $31 paywall

item.title: Preterm birth and low Vitamin D - many studies
item.description:
• Summary

Summary

Preterm birth rates are higher if

  • Low vitamin D

  • Low Omega-3

  • Poor vitamin D receptor

item.title: Vitamin D interactions with poor gut (Celiac, IBD, and Bariatric surgery) – several studies
